memoryArtifacts.headline: History of de Hoghton
memoryArtifacts.description: The title was created on 22 May 1611 for Richard Hoghton, Member of Parliament for Lancashire. The Hoghton family had been landowners in Lancashire since the reign of King Stephen and had been Knights …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Adam de Houghton IV於1225年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾霍格頓 堂爾時，他的父親Adam de Hocton II33歲，母親Agnes32歲。他於1260年之前與Aurelia在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。他去世於1290年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾霍頓 白 沃林頓享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adam de Houghton IV于1225年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾霍格頓 堂爾时，他的父亲Adam de Hocton II33岁，母亲Agnes32岁。他于1260年之前与Aurelia在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。他去世于1290年在英国英格兰藍卡爾霍頓 白 沃林頓享年65岁，埋葬在英国英格兰藍卡爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Houghton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of numerous places called Houghton or Hoghton. Most of the placenames derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some have different etymologies; for example, Little Houghton in Eccles (Lancashire), which derives from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn, and Houghton in Bigbury (Devon), which may derive from an Old English personal name Huhha + Old English tūn.

variants.name: Haughton
meanings: English: habitational name from Haughton (Cheshire, Lancashire, Northumberland, Shropshire, Staffordshire), or Haughton le Skerne (Durham). The placenames derive from Old English halh ‘nook, corner of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .
Irish: adopted for Ó hEacháin ‘descendant of Eachán’, a pet form of the personal name Eachaidh (see Hoey ).
Irish: adopted for Ó hEachtair ‘descendant of Eachtair’, a personal name of uncertain origin. Compare Hoctor .

variants.name: Hufton
meanings: English: habitational name; probably from Stony Houghton in Pleasley (Derbyshire), the name of which derives from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Houghton .

nameMeanings.name: Adam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Adam de Houghton IV was born in 1225, in Hoghton Tower, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Adam de Hocton II, was 33 and his mother, Agnes, was 32. He married Aurelia before 1260, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. He died in 1290, in Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Engl: Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adam de Houghton IV est né en 1225, à Hoghton Tower, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Adam de Hocton II, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Agnes, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Aurelia avant 1260, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Il est décédé en 1290, à Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adam de Houghton IV 1225 in Hoghton Tower, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam de Hocton II 33 und seine Mutter Agnes 32. Er hat vor 1260 in Europa Aurelia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Er starb 1290 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Aurelia prima del 1260, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. ja.lifeSummary: Adam de Houghton IVが1225年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアホートン トウエルで生まれた時，父Adam de Hocton IIは33歳，母Agnesは32歳であった。1260年以前にヨーロッパでAureliaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼は1290年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアホーウトン ビー ワーリントンで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスランカシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 허그h턴 비 워링턴
ko.lifeSummary: Adam de Houghton IV(이)가 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 호그h턴 타워에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam de Hocton II(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1260년 이전 우럽에서 Aurelia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1290년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 허그h턴 비 워링턴에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adam de Houghton IV nasceu em 1225, em Hoghton Tower, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Adam de Hocton II, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Agnes, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Aurelia antes 1260, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1290, em Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Houghton by Warrington, Lancashire, Engl: хоугтон би варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adam de Houghton IV родился в 1225 г. в местности хогтон тоуир, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Adam de Hocton II было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Aurelia перед 1260 г. в местности Европа. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Он умер в 1290 г. в местности хоугтон би варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.
